title: Convergence of Death Traditions
description: The structural convergence beneath the cultural differences of death traditions. All six mapped traditions (Tibetan, Egyptian, Steiner, Tantraloka, Layayoga, Daimon path) describe the same six-phase arc: death dissolution → purification → encounter with totality → self-judgment → transition → rebirth or liberation. The number 12 appears across traditions as hours, gates, deity-days, Kālīs, zodiacal spheres, and cakra petals.
